description: Disciplined Docker Compose deploy on a single Linux VM (Hetzner / Strato / Hosteurope / generic VPS). DECLARATIVE STUB — no runtime client shipped. Describes the disciplined subtype: registry-backed images with immutable semver tags, no `:latest`. Setups using mutable tags or local-build-on-server are out of scope for this profile. Coolify / Dokku / Caprover / Kubernetes / Docker Swarm are sibling targets that may get their own profiles when implemented. Fields derived from public best-practice articles accessed 2026-04-30.

required_env_vars.name: DOCKER_REGISTRY_USER
required_env_vars.description: Image registry username (Docker Hub, GitHub Container Registry, GitLab Registry, etc.).

required_env_vars.name: DOCKER_REGISTRY_TOKEN
required_env_vars.description: Image registry access token. NEVER store the token value in this profile — only the env-var name.

required_env_vars.name: SSH_USER
required_env_vars.description: SSH user with docker-compose privileges on the VM.

optional_env_vars.name: SSH_KEY_PATH
optional_env_vars.description: Path to SSH private key. Optional if ssh-agent is configured.

rollback.manual_procedure_reference: Update the image tag in docker-compose.yml on the VM (or via committed compose-file + redeploy), then: ssh {host} && docker compose pull && docker compose up -d --force-recreate. Old image tags remain in the registry as the rollback point.

known_gaps: This profile assumes registry-backed immutable image tags (semver). Setups that build on the server or use `:latest` are out of scope — they violate Pattern 1 (Revertable Deploys) by definition.
auto_trigger is 'off' because plain Docker Compose has no built-in health-check rollback. Add Watchtower, Argus, or a CI/CD hook for auto-rollback.
Coolify / Dokku / Caprover / Portainer wrap docker-compose with their own deployment models — they should get sibling profiles (`coolify-vps`, `dokku-vps`, etc.) rather than reusing this one.
Multi-host / Docker Swarm / Kubernetes are entirely different orchestration models, not covered.
Provenance recording relies on git history of the docker-compose.yml file + registry image-tag history — there is no centralized deploy-event log unless you wire one up externally.
